learning_outcomes.description: Apply knowledge of oral diseases to explain clinical observations and to solve clinical problems.
Demonstrate competence in the procedural skills required for the practice of minimal intervention dentistry.
Demonstrate an applied knowledge of dental material properties and their application to direct restorative techniques.
Appropriately select and use instrumentation and dental materials for the practice of minimal intervention dentistry.
Apply the principles of clinical communication, behaviour management and oral health education to the clinical setting.
Demonstrate professional conduct, including while working with other members of the dental team.

assessments.assessment_title: Assessment Item 1- Restorative Practical: Class I & V
assessments.task_description:
In this pre-clinical assessment, you will be allocated time within your pre-clinic time under exam conditions, to complete a specified number of Class I and V cavity preparations and/or restorations on the teeth identified to you on the day of the assessment. You must bring all PPE, restorative instruments, pre-clinic lab coat and be dressed professionally in DMD navy scrubs.

The rubric used for the marking of this assessment will be available in Blackboard. Performance parameters, and assessment criteria and standards will be made available on the course Blackboard site prior to the assessment.

This assessment task is not eligible for a re-mark as it will be assessed by two or more examiners and post-marking moderation process will be applied.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.assessment_title: Assessment Item 2- Cariology, Restorative & Material Science Examination
assessments.task_description: This task will be an invigilated written examination held during week 7 Wednesday lecture time. It will consist of a number of short answer, long answer and multi-part questions related to the module on cariology, restorative dentistry and material science.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.due_date:
7/10/2026 - 28/10/2026

Weeks 10-13 of peer clinic.

Rostered groups with LA.
assessments.task_description:
During your radiography module, you will need to demonstrate the safe competency in taking bitewings and periapical radiographs in the peer clinic setting using the Dexter manikin. This will be assessed during your rostered peer clinic sessions, where attendance is mandatory. This competency will include intraoral film holders, orientations, as well as the bisecting angle and paralleling techniques.

The rubric used for the marking of this assessment will be available in Blackboard. Performance parameters, and assessment criteria and standards will be made available on the course Blackboard site prior to the assessment.

This assessment task is not eligible for a re-mark as the mode of assessment results in insufficient reviewable evidence of student achievement.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Will be submitted from the Radiology clinic once allocated student session is completed.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.due_date:
7/10/2026 - 28/10/2026

Weeks 10-13 of peer clinic.

Rostered groups with Radiography.
assessments.task_description:
You will need to demonstrate the safe competency in the administration of LA during your peer clinic sessions. You will need to demonstrate sound infection control practices, the mantling and dismantling of a LA syringe, and the administration of an infiltration and an inferior alveolar nerve (with lingual) block using the correct anatomical knowledge and clinical procedure. You will also need to show palatal infiltration intraoral injection points, without needing to administer the local anaesthetic for this injection.

The rubric used for the marking of this assessment will be available in Blackboard. Performance parameters, and assessment criteria and standards will be made available on the course Blackboard site prior to the assessment.

This assessment task is not eligible for a re-mark as the mode of assessment results in insufficient reviewable evidence of student achievement.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.assessment_title: Assessment Item 7- Clinical Case-based Viva Voce
assessments.task_description:
You will review a written case (15 minutes), then participate in a 15-minute oral examination with 2-3 examiners. The discussion will assess your understanding and application of restorative dentistry principles and material science, clinical diagnoses with reasoning, simple treatment planning, and application of evidence-based practice. Bring writing materials such as a pen and highlighter and please wear professional attire or DMD navy scrubs. No electronic devices or additional resources permitted. Further details will be provided on the course Blackboard site.

This assessment task is not eligible for a re-mark as it will be assessed by two or more examiners and post-marking moderation process will be applied.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description: This written examination will occur in the end of semester exam period and consists of multiple choice questions, short, multi-part and long answer questions.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.topic:
Pre-clinic

Three (3) pre-clinic sessions per week where students will learn fundamental restorative dentistry skills.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06
learning_activities.topic_description: Pre-clinic Three (3) pre-clinic sessions per week where students will learn fundamental restorative dentistry skills.

learning_activities.topic:
Lecture

Two (2) lectures per week to deliver course content.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06
learning_activities.topic_description: Lecture Two (2) lectures per week to deliver course content.

learning_activities.topic:
Peer clinic

There is one (1) peer clinic session per week with peer-to-peer activities preparing you for the patient clinic.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06
learning_activities.topic_description: Peer clinic There is one (1) peer clinic session per week with peer-to-peer activities preparing you for the patient clinic.

learning_activities.topic:
Tutorial

One (1) tutorial session per week, to practice applying the knowledge taught throughout the course via lectures and self-directed learning activities to clinical patient scenarios and clinical-based learning activities.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06
learning_activities.topic_description: Tutorial One (1) tutorial session per week, to practice applying the knowledge taught throughout the course via lectures and self-directed learning activities to clinical patient scenarios and clinical-based learning activities.
